article_title: UK Imposes First Conduct Requirements on Google Under New Digital Markets Regime, Targeting AI Overviews and Search Dominance

content_preview.title: UK Imposes First Conduct Requirements on Google Under New Digital Markets Regime, Targeting AI Overviews and Search Dominance
content_preview.summary: The CMA proposed its first conduct requirements under the UK's new digital markets law, targeting Google's AI Overviews, search ranking, and choice screens, while securing app store commitments from Apple and Google effective April 1.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The United Kingdom's Competition and Markets Authority has proposed four conduct requirements for Google's search services — the first ever issued under the Digital Markets, Competition and Consumers Act 2024 — that would force the company to give publishers meaningful controls over how their content is used in AI-generated search features, expand choice screens across Android and Chrome, guarantee at least 30 business days' notice before material ranking changes, and formalize data...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong regulatory Analysis with detailed policy comparison (UK vs EU DMA). Well-structured with four specific conduct requirements.
editor_notes.source_verification: gov.uk CMA (fetched): Confirms first conduct requirements, DMCCA 2024, four obligations (publisher opt-out, fair ranking, choice screens, data portability), SMS designation Oct 2025, 90%+ market share. gov.uk app store (fetched): Confirms Apple/Google commitments April 1 2026. TechCrunch: EU antitrust context.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified against official UK government sources.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and analytical. Good comparative framing without bias.
editor_notes.originality: No prior UK digital markets regime coverage.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality regulatory analysis. Ready for publication.
